#  Enable or Disable Specification Areas 
##  Things to Consider 
  * [Required User Permissions](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/specifications-project/permissions)
  * **Additional Information:**
    * You cannot disable specification areas if there is more than one specification area in the Specifications tool. To disable specification areas, first [delete all specification areas](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/specifications-project/tutorials/delete-a-specification-area) until you have only one, and then disable the setting by following the steps in the Disable Specification Areas section.


##  Steps 
### Enable Specification Areas
  1. Navigate to the project's **Specifications** tool.
  2. Click the **Configure Settings** ![Icon Settings Gear Grey3](https://images.ctfassets.net/plvbd9i6qkpm/6649669377546a67a1cc36afbe2e5bbf/2b17fe103fb52d67228f09a9458adfd7/icon-settings-gear-grey3.png?fm=webp&h=20) icon.
  3. Click **Yes** to enable the feature.
  4. Click **Save**.


### Disable Specification Areas
**_Important!_** You cannot disable specification areas if there is more than one specification area in the Specifications tool. To disable specification areas, first [delete all specification areas](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/specifications-project/tutorials/delete-a-specification-area) until you have only one, and then disable the setting by following the steps below.
  1. Navigate to the project's **Specifications** tool.
  2. Click the **Configure Settings** ![Icon Settings Gear Grey3](https://images.ctfassets.net/plvbd9i6qkpm/6649669377546a67a1cc36afbe2e5bbf/2b17fe103fb52d67228f09a9458adfd7/icon-settings-gear-grey3.png?fm=webp&h=20) icon.
  3. Click **No** to disable the feature.
  4. Click **Save**.


## See Also
  * [Add a Specification Area](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/specifications-project/tutorials/add-a-specification-area)
  * [Configure Advanced Settings: Specifications](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/specifications-project/tutorials/configure-advanced-settings-specifications)
  * [What gets copied over to a new project from a project template?](https://v2.support.procore.com/faq-what-gets-copied-over-to-a-new-project-when-applying-a-project-template)
title: Enable or Disable Specification Areas
description: How to enable Specification Areas for a project so users can add and create different specification areas to categorize specifications.
